errors.description: Platform-admin surface reference to a non-existent SA. Never surfaces to token-exchange callers — those errors are always folded into the generic INVALID_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS response (enumeration safety).
Creation attempt collided with an existing non-deleted SA on the same `application_id`.
The `application_id` in the create payload does not exist or is disabled.
The proposed `tenant_id` belongs to an application other than the SA's `application_id`. Cross-application tenant scope is forbidden.
PATCH attempted to change `application_id`, `tenant_id`, or `signer_kid`. Response body names the offending field(s).

The SA's `is_enabled = false`. Runtime rejection at service_account.verify_jwt or the token-exchange endpoint. NEVER surfaced to the machine caller — folded into the generic 401 to preserve enumeration safety.

The SA's `expires_at < now()` with no rotation. Token-exchange refuses; existing JWTs signed before expiry remain valid until natural exp. Response body folded into generic INVALID_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS for enumeration safety.

Generic token-exchange failure: unknown SA-ID, wrong secret, disabled SA, expired secret — all four cases return this single payload with no distinguishing signal. Audit-log entry captures the specific reason for triage.

Runtime JWT rejection at service_account.verify_jwt. Any of: signature mismatch, unknown kid, kid disabled, jti in deny-list, `iss`/`aud` mismatch, exp elapsed, iat too far in future. Same generic error regardless of which check failed (enumeration safety); the audit log captures which check failed for triage.
JWT's `app` claim does not match the receiving service's `config('app.slug')`. Downstream service rejection; the request never reaches business logic.
JWT's `tid` claim does not match the request's `X-Tenant-Id` header (or the routed tenant context).

Enterprise-only. When `service_accounts_ip_allowlist` entitlement is active AND the SA has a non-empty allowlist in metadata, requests from non-allowlisted source IPs refuse with 403. Deferred to Wave 1b enforcement; the error code is reserved here for wire stability.
Token-exchange or IssueTestJwtInput requested a TTL above `service-accounts.jwt.max_ttl_seconds` (default 3600). Enterprise callers with `service_accounts_extended_ttl` entitlement bypass this.
Token-exchange or IssueTestJwtInput requested a TTL below `service-accounts.jwt.min_ttl_seconds` (default 60). Prevents caller-driven exchange-endpoint pressure via near-zero TTLs.

Token-exchange endpoint refused by rate-limit middleware. 5/min per SA-ID OR 20/min per source /24. Response includes Retry-After header. Sustained spikes fire SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification via the fingerprinter.
Naming rule `valid_service_account_name` refused: too long, invalid characters, missing alphanumeric bookends.

The proposed `signer_kid` does not exist in `auth::jwt_signing_keys` OR is disabled. Callers who don't specify a kid at creation get a fresh kid provisioned; explicit-kid callers must reference an active row.

The proposed `signer_kid` is bound to an application other than the SA's `application_id`. Kids are optionally application-scoped in the auth module; SAs must reference a kid within their own application.
Requested capability requires a paid-plan entitlement. Response body names the missing key (`service_accounts_platform_plane`, `service_accounts_ip_allowlist`, `service_accounts_extended_ttl`).
Mutating command invoked without --reason=<text> while `service-accounts.commands.require_reason` is on.
Attempted to hard-delete an SA still inside the retention hold. Wait for the hold to expire OR use `service-account:early-purge` (super_admin + compliance co-sign, deferred CLI extension).
